result:
If I could change the world in one way, it wouldn’t be through a grand technological or political shift—but through a subtle, pervasive adjustment in human perception.  

I’d want everyone to truly *feel* the interconnectedness of things—not just as an abstract idea, but as an immediate, empathetic understanding. To viscerally grasp that another person’s suffering is your own, that harm to an ecosystem is harm to yourself, that a stranger’s joy can resonate in your own life.  

That shift in awareness would dissolve many of the illusions that fuel greed, prejudice, indifference, and environmental destruction. It wouldn’t erase all conflict—differences and tensions are part of being human—but it would strip away the *unnecessary* suffering born from separation and blindness to our shared existence.  

In essence: I’d change the root of perception, so that compassion isn’t something we have to remind ourselves of, but the default state of being.
